summary: The original mongodb-sales-aggregation-engine grader ran the trusted pytest suite, which imports the untrusted candidate solution (/app/query.py) INTO THE SAME interpreter that produces the verdict. With a root candidate in-container, that gives the candidate full in-process control of the verdict. The fix adopts the harden-v0/Harbor convention: run the candidate OUT-OF-PROCESS and grade its outputs in a verdict process that never imports candidate code.

fix_implemented.grader: v6 out-of-process grader (artifacts/chronos/demo/publish/v2-env/grader/grade.py + run_solution.py + reference_query.py). env.py runs `python3 -I /tests/grader/grade.py`: for each case it seeds the DB, runs the candidate and the TRUSTED reference solution in throwaway isolated subprocesses, and compares their JSON outputs. The verdict process imports no candidate code; its exit code is the reward. The grader files are digest-pinned at grade time.
fix_implemented.convention_source: harden-v0 delegates grading to Harbor, which runs the verifier as a SEPARATE process writing a reward FILE (never the candidate's exit code), optionally in a SEPARATE verifier environment / as a non-agent user. See the harden-v0 investigation: harbor/verifier/verifier.py, harbor/trial/trial.py:_run_separate_verifier, harden/instructions.py:291.

bypass_classes.mechanism: /app/query.py registers atexit -> os._exit(0) to override a failing exit code
bypass_classes.v6_out_of_process: blocked (verdict process never imports candidate; re-proven: exit_code_hijack_blocked)

bypass_classes.mechanism: candidate rewrites os/subprocess/pytest/xml internals in the verdict process
bypass_classes.v3_v4_v5: could still bypass (candidate ran in the verdict process)
bypass_classes.v6_out_of_process: blocked (candidate runs only in throwaway subprocesses; re-proven: monkeypatch_blocked)

bypass_classes.mechanism: root candidate overwrites the grader/reference files or plants a shadowing stdlib module
bypass_classes.v6_out_of_process: blocked (grader files digest-pinned; runner invoked with python3 -I; re-proven: root_grader_tamper_blocked)

bypass_classes.mechanism: a root candidate reads the grader + reference + the FIXED test cases and hard-codes the expected outputs keyed by the (fixed) args
bypass_classes.v6_out_of_process: RESIDUAL — not defeated by an out-of-process grader alone. Requires randomised per-run inputs (so precomputed answers do not match) and/or test data hidden from the candidate (a non-root candidate or a SEPARATE verifier environment). This is an env/Plan-005 hardening, recorded for follow-up.

shared_with_releaseproof: The sealed Plan 005 ReleaseProof grader (grader_v2_digest c0d86ae) is the in-process pytest form and is subject to the in-process bypass classes. The v6 out-of-process grader supersedes it for the deployed env and verifies the same functional behaviour by output comparison against the reference.
deploy_status: v6 is implemented and re-proven (artifacts/chronos/demo/publish/v2-deploy-proof.json: deploy_form_pass with witness/exit-hijack/monkeypatch/grader-tamper all blocked and controls preserved). The LIVE published env is still v5 (XML grader: blocks exit-hijack but NOT monkeypatch) pending a v6 deploy decision.
remaining_to_fully_close: Deploy v6, then add randomised/hidden test data (or a non-root candidate / SEPARATE verifier environment) to close the answer-hardcoding residual.
